currentUse: Vacant former worship site; closed by the Diocese of Allentown after the final Mass on January 5, 2026, with no publicly announced sale, buyer, or reuse plan found as of July 2026
summary: St. Vincent de Paul Church at 2nd and C Streets in Girardville ceased functioning as a Catholic worship site on January 5, 2026, when the final Mass was celebrated, the bells tolled around 7 PM, and the lights were extinguished around 8 PM, ending over a century of Catholic worship at the site and the Catholic Church's brick-and-mortar presence in Girardville. The Diocese of Allentown approved the closure in December 2025 at the request of St. Charles Borromeo Parish in Ashland, which had overseen the site since the 2015 merger of the Ashland, Girardville, and Gordon parishes; regular weekend Masses had already ended in 2021. The diocese cited financial and structural challenges: roughly $250,000 needed in exterior repairs, unsafe interior conditions, aging roofs, and about $20,000 in annual operating costs. Both the Finance and Pastoral Councils of St. Charles Borromeo voted to support the closure, and all liturgies relocated to the main church in Ashland. The brick Gothic revival building — which retained Lithuanian stained-glass windows, a Lithuanian flag, and the Vytis cross symbol into its final years — remains standing, but no sale, demolition, or repurposing plan has been publicly reported since the closure.

developments.headline: Diocese of Allentown confirms St. Vincent de Paul will close in January 2026
developments.detail: The Diocese of Allentown confirmed the closure following a request from St. Charles Borromeo Parish in Ashland, citing an estimated $250,000 in needed exterior repairs, unsafe interior conditions, and roughly $20,000 in annual operating costs. A closing Mass was scheduled for January 5, 2026, with a final Christmas Eve Mass on December 24, 2025. The diocese stated it 'recognizes the emotional significance of Saint Vincent de Paul Church for the Girardville community.'

sources.title: Saint Vincent de Paul Church in Girardville Set to Close in January 2026

developments.headline: Closure detailed a decade after the 2015 merger that created St. Charles Borromeo
developments.detail: The Shenandoah Sentinel reported that St. Vincent de Paul had been retained as a worship site after the 2015 merger of the Catholic parishes of Ashland, Girardville, and Gordon into St. Charles Borromeo Parish, but regular weekend Masses ended in 2021 and the building saw very limited use afterward. Both the parish Finance and Pastoral Councils voted to support closing the site, and all liturgies were relocated to the main church in Ashland.

sources.title: Decade after merger, Diocese closing worship site in Girardville

developments.headline: Final Mass; bells toll and lights go out after more than 100 years
developments.detail: On January 5, 2026, the final Mass was celebrated at St. Vincent de Paul Church at 2nd and C Streets. Around 7 PM the church bells tolled for the last time, and at about 8 PM the lights were extinguished, ending the Catholic Church's physical presence in Girardville. The Sentinel recounted that the parish began in 1906 in a renovated former Armory, replaced by the current building constructed in 1923 and consecrated in November 1925.

sources.title: Lights go out on longtime Girardville church

conflictsWithArchiveRecord: Two refinements to the archive record. (1) Founding date: the archive says founded 1907, but both SkookNews and the Shenandoah Sentinel state the parish began in 1906, when members purchased and renovated a former Armory; the present church was built in 1923 on the same site and consecrated in November 1925 (Global True Lithuania gives 1926 as the build year — sources disagree by 1-3 years on the current building's date). (2) Final Mass time: SkookNews (Dec 8, 2025) announced the closing Mass for 5:00 PM on January 5, 2026, while the Sentinel's day-of report describes the final Mass at 6 PM — the date itself (Jan 5, 2026) matches the archive exactly. Everything else confirms the archive: Lithuanian heritage parish, 2015 merger into St. Charles Borromeo, diocesan closure as a worship site, Diocese of Allentown (coal region).
gaps: No information found on the property's post-closure disposition: no sale listing, sale price, buyer, canonical decree of relegation to profane use, demolition permit, or preservation campaign appeared in searches through July 2026 (searched: 'St. Vincent de Paul Girardville sale/demolition/for sale', 'relegation decree profane use', and month-scoped 2026 news queries). The building's exact street address is inconsistently reported (Sentinel: '2nd and C Streets'; a mass-times aggregator lists 260 North 2nd Street — the aggregator page was not independently opened, so the address is not asserted in the summary). Who officiated the final Mass was not named in the coverage found. No reporting found on the fate of the Lithuanian stained-glass windows or other heritage artifacts after closure.
